en.extract:
The Angola swallow (Hirundo angolensis) is a species of swallow that is native to the Afrotropics.


Description
They measure 15 cm and weigh 16 to 19 g. The plumage of the forehead, throat and upper breast is coloured deep rufous-chestnut. The crown and upperparts are a shiny steel-blue. Flight and tail feathers are black, the latter with large white windows.


Behaviour
They frequent a variety of open habitats up to the fringes of forest, whether altered by man or natural. Their diet consists of a variety of flying insects. They may forage alone or in flocks, and emit a weak twittering. Their breeding season and abundance depends much on their region of residence, and a few undertake migrations.


Range
It is found in Angola, Burundi, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Gabon, Kenya, Malawi, Namibia, Rwanda, Tanzania, Uganda, and Zambia.


Relationships
It has been considered conspecific with the Red-chested swallow, but is generally taken as a full species in a species complex that includes Barn, Red-chested, Pacific, Welcome, White-throated and Ethiopian swallows.


Subspecies
There are two subspecies, but intermediate forms occur:

H. a. angolensis – type from Huíla Province, Angola
H. a. arcticincta Sharpe, 1891 – East Africa


Gallery


References

The '''Angola swallow''' ('''''Hirundo angolensis''''') is a species of [[Hirundinidae|swallow]] that is native to the [[Afrotropical realm|Afrotropics]].

==Description==
They measure 15 cm and weigh 16 to 19 g. The plumage of the forehead, throat and upper breast is coloured deep rufous-chestnut. The crown and upperparts are a shiny steel-blue. Flight and tail feathers are black, the latter with large white windows.<ref name="tur">{{cite web|last1=Turner|first1=A.|title=Angolan Swallow (Hirundo angolensis)|url=http://www.hbw.com/species/angolan-swallow-hirundo-angolensis|website=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive|publisher=Lynx Edicions, Barcelona|access-date=9 April 2016}}</ref>

==Behaviour==
They frequent a variety of open habitats up to the fringes of forest, whether altered by man or natural. Their diet consists of a variety of flying insects. They may forage alone or in flocks, and emit a weak twittering. Their breeding season and abundance depends much on their region of residence, and a few undertake migrations.<ref name="tur"/>

==Range==
It is found in [[Angola]], [[Burundi]], [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]], [[Gabon]], [[Kenya]], [[Malawi]], [[Namibia]], [[Rwanda]], [[Tanzania]], [[Uganda]], and [[Zambia]].

==Relationships==
It has been considered conspecific with the [[Red-chested swallow]], but is generally taken as a full species in a species complex that includes [[Hirundo rustica|Barn]], [[Hirundo lucida|Red-chested]], [[Hirundo tahitica|Pacific]], [[Hirundo neoxena|Welcome]], [[Hirundo albigularis|White-throated]] and [[Hirundo aethiopica|Ethiopian swallows]].<ref name="ibc">{{cite web|title=Angolan Swallow (Hirundo angolensis) - HBW 9, p. 666|url=http://ibc.lynxeds.com/species/angolan-swallow-hirundo-angolensis|website=The Internet Bird Collection (IBC)|access-date=9 April 2016}}</ref>

==Subspecies==
There are two subspecies, but intermediate forms occur:<ref name="ibc"/>
* ''H. a. angolensis'' – type from [[Huíla Province]], Angola
* ''H. a. arcticincta'' <small>Sharpe, 1891</small><ref name="avi">{{cite web|title=Angola Swallow (arcticincta) |url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?lang=EN&avibaseid=B6072512 |website=avibase |access-date=9 April 2016}}</ref> – East Africa

==Gallery==

Hirundo angolensis 1894.jpg|Nominate subsp. from illustration by [[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|R. B. Sharpe]] (1894)
Hirundo angolensis, arcticincta race 1894.jpg|Subsp. ''arcticincta'' with deeper tail-fork & paler belly plumage
Image: Hirundo angolensis MHNT.jpg|Egg of Angola swallow
</gallery>
